criteria.reason: `src/App.tsx:1433-1551` now renders the left side as only the title/new-project header plus the scrolling project/session navigation area, with no extra footer or sidebar status chrome outside that navigation region.

`src/App.tsx:1462-1545` keeps project expand/collapse and `new-session-button`, while each `session-row` is reduced to the session name plus one status badge; the lifecycle flow still passed in `e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:127-141`.

`src/App.tsx:1587-1640` keeps only `active-session-label`, the Start/Stop/New Panel controls, one project chip, and the current session/agent chips, and fresh repo inspection found no remaining `session-runtime-context`, `session-sandbox-mode`, or `session-agent-url` nodes; `e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:135-137` and `:183-185` now pass their absence checks.

`src/styles.css:30-34` and `src/App.tsx:1433-1453` plus `:1554-1649` constrain the shell to full height and route scrolling into inner regions, and a fresh 1440x900 Playwright verifier measured `pageScrollHeight=900`, `viewportHeight=900`, `overflowX=auto`, and `overflowY=hidden` for `session-panel-scroll-region`.

`src/App.tsx:402-420` and `:730-770` focus or create the matching editor, call `scrollPanelIntoView` on that editor panel, and prevent duplicate editor panels for the same file; `e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:247-264` passed the one-editor-per-file flow and updated `editor-active-path` on reopen/switch.

`src/App.tsx:1143-1169` keeps the editor title, visible `editor-active-path`, and save/close controls in the main header, while `src/components/editor-panel.tsx:57-74` renders only the scroll-contained editor body with no duplicate lower path/status strip; the editor-open regression still passed in `e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:252-255` and `:263-264`.

`src/App.tsx:1196-1210`, `src/components/terminal-panel.tsx:21-110`, and `server/web-backend.js:1019-1123` keep the terminal full-height, theme-backed with solid `bg-card`, started via the existing terminal API, resized via `ResizeObserver`, and stopped through the current session terminal stop path; a fresh Playwright verifier confirmed xterm mounted, the terminal background resolved to `rgb(255, 255, 255)`, and typed input was echoed back through the live integration.

`src/App.tsx:98-113`, `:279-280`, `:708-716`, and `:1333-1379` drive browser navigation entirely from the top URL row, normalize URLs, submit on Enter, and render `web-open-button` only when the normalized draft differs from the loaded URL; a fresh Playwright verifier navigated by pressing Enter, saw the iframe load `http://localhost:43111/api/health`, then confirmed the button disappeared until the draft changed again.

`src/App.tsx:210`, `:386-397`, `:906-948`, `:1034-1050`, and `:1651-1730` keep per-session width state, pointer-driven resizing, focus retention, and drag/drop ordering in the panel rail; `e2e/workspace-panel-polish.spec.ts:62-116` passed the resize-plus-horizontal-scroll reorder regression, while `e2e/usage-recordings.spec.ts:116-121`, `:247-319`, and the fresh terminal/browser verifier showed close, file-open, browser, and terminal flows still working in the same session.

commands_run.notes: `vite build` succeeded; Vite emitted only the existing non-failing chunk-size warning.
Playwright ran the 3 usage-recording regressions and all passed.
Playwright passed the panel resize + horizontal-scroll drag-reorder regression.

A fresh host-mode verifier created a session at 1440x900, confirmed there was no page-level vertical overflow, verified the terminal mounted with a solid theme background and echoed typed input, and verified Enter-based browser navigation plus the conditional `web-open-button` behavior.

caveats: AC1-AC3 and part of AC5-AC7 rely on current source inspection alongside the fresh passing Playwright coverage, because the existing automated suite does not snapshot every visual simplification directly.
The focused verifier was run as an inline Playwright/Node harness instead of a checked-in spec file to avoid modifying production or evidence files during verification.
